what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard breakdown item that records a design concept for an intelligent, typed, addressable document tree (an AST over rendered markdown/HTML) to support smart selection, commenting, navigation, and collapse/expand.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id item-b214f58b, address board://item-b214f58b, type block, source claude_code, channel dragnet-development, and a part_of link to board://item-11da929d.
The body is titled 'Breakdown · Intelligent block structure (the document tree / types)'.
It starts from a SPECIFIED requirement: selection should be intelligent (bullet vs list vs paragraph vs heading vs everything under a heading).
It infers an IMPLIED document model: a semantic tree of typed nodes (heading, paragraph, sentence, word, list, list-item, quote, code-block, table, row, cell, image, zone).
Headings OWN content beneath them until the next same-or-higher heading; lists own items; paragraphs own sentences own words.
Every node must be addressable via a stable path/address.
It explicitly says this goes beyond the current flat block list.
It extends the same tree thinking to UI (element < component < section < page).

ADDITIONS list features: collapse/expand subtrees, breadcrumbs, comment on a node and its children, rollup of comments under a heading, tree-powered navigation/jump-to-node, and per-type default comment behaviors.
standouts: The core proposal is an addressable semantic AST, not a flat block list.
Headings own subsequent content until the next peer-or-higher heading.
Every node has a stable address/path.
The same tree abstraction is applied to both documents and UI.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Uses board:// URI identifiers for items and part_of links.
Markdown body uses a level-1 title matching the frontmatter title.
Structures design reasoning into SPECIFIED / IMPLIED / ADDITIONS sections.
Breaks from a flat-block model by proposing a hierarchical, typed document tree.
concerns: The term 'intelligent' is not operationally defined.
No concrete node type schema, path format, or addressing scheme is provided.
'Robust across markdown/HTML formats' is asserted but not detailed.
Per-type default comment behaviors are mentioned but not enumerated.
There are no acceptance criteria or implementation boundaries.
notes: A design-note entry proposing a semantic document tree with stable node addresses as the foundation for intelligent selection and annotation.
questions: What is the exact node type schema and which types are mandatory vs optional?
How are stable node paths/addresses generated and preserved across edits?
Which input formats are supported and how is markdown/HTML normalized into the tree?
How does ownership behave around heading-level skips (e.g. h2 followed by h4)?
What does 'per-type default comment behaviors' mean in practice?
How does this relate to the existing flat block list and migration path?
What is the scope of BD-C and the parent item item-11da929d?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a breakdown of intelligent block structure, and the file delivers exactly that design breakdown.
apparent_themes: Unified tree model for documents and UI
Selection and annotation anchored to typed nodes
Navigation and discovery via the document tree
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a unified, addressable tree model of documents (and by extension UI) so that selections, comments, and navigation can operate on semantically meaningful subtrees rather than flat blocks.
novelty: It treats rendered content as a typed, addressable AST where headings own everything beneath them until the next peer-or-higher heading, and applies the same tree abstraction to UI elements.
contribution: The concept of a typed, addressable document tree with heading-based ownership, plus the derived feature set: collapse/expand, breadcrumbs, node-and-children comments, comment rollups, jump-to-node navigation, and per-type comment defaults.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design breakdown proposing a semantic, typed, addressable document tree (AST) to replace flat block lists and enable intelligent selection, commenting, and navigation.
intention_for_embedding: To define the document model and interaction patterns needed for intelligent block-level selection and annotation in a content/commenting system.
